overview.minides: MG's flagship ICE SUV — a Gloster successor with segment-first triple differential locks, M-Crawl, 12-speaker JBL audio, 12-way massaging front seats, and 64-colour ambient lighting under ₹45 lakh.
overview.description: Launched on 27 May 2026, the MG Majestor replaces the Gloster as MG India's flagship ICE SUV. Built on the Maxus D90 body-on-frame platform, it is powered by the same 2.0L twin-turbo diesel (215 hp / 479 Nm) + 8-speed automatic seen in the Gloster's top trims. Offered in two trim levels — Sharp (RWD only, pricing TBA) and Savvy (₹40.99L–₹44.99L) — the Majestor measures 5046 mm in length with a 2950 mm wheelbase, making it the largest SUV in its segment. Available in 6-seater (captain seats) or 7-seater (bench) second-row configs. The Savvy gets dual 12.3-inch displays, a panoramic sunroof, three-zone climate control, 12-way powered + heated + ventilated + massaging front seats, second-row massaging seats, 64-colour ambient lighting, dual wireless chargers, 12-speaker JBL audio, Level 2 ADAS, 360-degree camera, powered tailgate, and a 4WD system with segment-first triple differential locks + M-Crawl + 10 off-road terrain modes. Boot space is 343 litres (all rows up) expandable to 1250 litres.

dimensions.bootSpace: 343 L (all seats up) / 1250 L (3rd row folded)

suspension.front: Independent Double Wishbone with Coil Springs
suspension.rear: Rigid Axle with Coil Springs (4×2) / Solid Axle (4×4)
